summary: deploy.sh: auto-run app migrations before symlink swap
description:
Add a 'run app migrations' step to deploy/deploy.sh, executed in the FULL release directory ($RELEASE) BEFORE the symlink swap. Caught during first manual deploy 2026-04-22: the Next.js standalone build doesn't include scripts/ or migrations/, so 'npm run migrate' from /srv/guru-web/current (which symlinks to .next/standalone/) fails with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for scripts/migrate.ts.

Two implementation options:

A) cd $RELEASE && DATABASE_URL=$(grep ^DATABASE_URL /etc/guru-web.env | cut -d= -f2-) npm run migrate
   - Uses the existing tsx-based scripts/migrate.ts
   - Single line in deploy.sh
   - Reads DATABASE_URL from /etc/guru-web.env (which deploy user can read? check perms — currently 600 root:root, so NO. Either add deploy to group with read access, OR use a different env source.)

B) for f in $RELEASE/migrations/*.sql; do sudo -u postgres psql -d guru -f "$f"; done
   - No DATABASE_URL needed — psql connects locally as postgres superuser
   - Doesn't depend on tsx/Node tooling
   - Requires deploy user to have NOPASSWD sudo for psql or use postgres user directly

Option B is simpler and matches how migrations were applied manually. But it skips whatever scripts/migrate.ts adds (transactions, ordering checks, idempotency tracking).

Decision required: how does scripts/migrate.ts differ from raw psql application? If it's just a thin wrapper, B is fine. If it tracks applied migrations in a meta table, A is the right call.

SCOPE: app schema only (users, sessions, queries, user_preferences, quota). Never touches the corpus tables (chunks, traditions, texts, concepts, edges) — those come from guru-pipeline's pg_restore and are out of scope for this script.

DISCIPLINE: this enables every push to apply schema changes. Destructive migrations (DROP, ALTER … TYPE) must be code-reviewed with care. Additive only is the safe default.
